items.remarks: NORMAL position may be inoperative provided the system is operative in the HIGH position.
HIGH position may be inoperative provided the system is operative in the NORMAL position.

item.remarks: One or both may be inoperative.

items.detail: (ATR72-212A '600 Version' with Mod. 07696, ATR72-212A '600F Version' with Mod. 07696)
items.remarks:
(M)(O) May be inoperative provided:
a) Both Packs are verified operative, and
b) ERACKV is verified operative.

(M)(O) May be inoperative provided:
a) One Pack is verified operative,
b) Emergency Ram Air Check Valve (ERACKV) is verified operative,
c) Flight is conducted at 17,000 ft. MSL or below, and
d) Extended Operations are not conducted.

equipment.name: Emergency Ram Air Check Valve (ERACKV) (ATR72-212A “600 Version” with Mod. 07696, ATR72-212A “600F Version” with Mod. 07696)

item.remarks: (M)(O) May be inoperative provided Emergency Ram Air Valve (ERAV) is verified operative.

(M)(O) May be inoperative provided:
a) Manual mode is verified to operate normally before each departure, and
b) Cabin differential pressure is maintained equal to or less than 1 psi.

(O) May be inoperative CLOSED provided:
a) EXHAUST MODE is selected to OVBD,
b) Flight is conducted at 17,000 ft. MSL or below, and
c) Adequate cooling is provided within 30 minutes after aircraft electrical system is powered on the ground.
NOTE: Not installed on cargo aircraft converted by STC ST01189WI.

(O) May be inoperative provided:
a) EXHAUST MODE is selected to OVBD,
b) Flight is conducted at 17,000 ft. MSL or below, and
c) Adequate cooling is provided within 30 minutes after aircraft electrical system is powered on the ground.

equipment.name: Cockpit Isolation Valve (Cargo Configuration STC ST01189WI)

item.remarks:
May be inoperative provided associated compartment remains empty or is verified to contain only empty cargo handling equipment, ballast (ballast may be loaded in ULDs), and/or Fly Away Kits.
NOTE: Operator MELs should define which items are approved for inclusion in the Fly Away Kits and which materials can be used as ballast.

items.remarks:
(M)(O) The pressurization system (automatic and manual modes) may be inoperative provided:
a) Flight is conducted in an unpressurized configuration with maximum operating altitude of 10,000 ft. MSL or below,
b) Outflow valve is secured in the open position,
c) Both packs are considered inoperative,
d) Operations are limited to one flight, and
e) Extended Operations are not conducted.
Refer to Item 21-50-1, Packs.

(M)(O) The pressurization system (automatic and manual modes) may be inoperative provided:
a) Flight is conducted in an unpressurized configuration with maximum operating altitude of 10,000 ft. MSL or below,
b) Outflow valve is secured in the open position,
c) One Pack and Bleed is verified operative, and
d) Extended Operations are not conducted.

items.detail: 2) Cargo Configuration (ATR72-212A “600 Version” with Mod. 07696, ATR72-212A “600F Version” with Mod. 07696)
items.remarks:
(M)(O) The pressurization system (automatic and manual modes) may be inoperative provided:
a) Flight is conducted in an unpressurized configuration with maximum operating altitude of 10,000 ft. MSL or below,
b) Outflow valve is secured in the open position,
c) Both packs are considered inoperative,
d) Procedures are established and used to ensure the cargo compartment remains empty or is verified to contain only empty cargo handling equipment, ballast (ballast may be loaded in ULDs), and/or Fly Away Kits,
e) Operations are limited to one flight, and
f) Extended Operations are not conducted.
NOTE: Operator MELs should define which items are approved for inclusion in the Fly Away Kits, and which materials can be used as ballast.
Refer to Item 21-50-1, Packs.

items.detail: 2) Cargo Configuration (ATR72-212A “600 Version” with Mod. 07696, ATR72-212A 600F Version with Mod. 07696)
items.remarks:
(M)(O) The pressurization system (automatic and manual modes) may be inoperative provided:
a) Flight is conducted in an unpressurized configuration with maximum operating altitude of 10,000 ft. MSL or below,
b) Outflow valve is secured in the open position,
c) One Pack and Bleed is verified operative, and
d) Extended Operations are not conducted.

equipment.name: Manual Pressurization Controller (All models except ATR72-212A “600 Version” with Mod. 07696 and ATR72-212A “600F Version” with Mod. 07696)

items.remarks:
(M)(O) May be inoperative provided:
a) Flight is conducted in an unpressurized configuration,
b) Extended overwater flight is prohibited, and
c) Repairs are made within 3 flight-days.

(M)(O) May be inoperative provided:
a) Flight is conducted in an unpressurized configuration, and
b) Procedures are established and used to ensure the cargo compartment remains empty or is verified to contain only empty cargo handling equipment, ballast (ballast may be loaded in ULDs), and/or Fly Away Kits.
NOTE: Operator MELs should define which items are approved for inclusion in the Fly Away Kits and which materials can be used as ballast.

items.detail: 1) (All models except ATR72-212A “600 Version” with Mod. 07696” and ATR72-212A “600F Version” with Mod. 07696)
items.remarks:
(O) May be inoperative provided:
a) Manual pressurization system is operative, and
b) ALT, RATE, and DIFF indicators operate normally.

items.detail: 1) (All models except ATR72-212A “600 Version” with Mod. 07696” and ATR72-212A “600F Version” with Mod. 07696)
items.remarks:
(M)(O) May be inoperative provided:
a) Flight is conducted in an unpressurized configuration,
b) Extended overwater flight is prohibited, and
c) Repairs are made within 3 flight-days.

items.remarks: (M) May be inoperative provided MAN mode is verified operative.

items.detail: 2) (ATR72-212A “600 Version” with Mod. 07696, ATR72-212A “600F Version” with Mod. 07696) c) MAN Mode
items.remarks: (M) May be inoperative provided Automatic Pressurization Control (NORM and BK UP) is verified operative.

item.remarks: May be inoperative.

(M)(O) May be inoperative provided:
a) Manual pressurization system is operative, and
b) Overboard valve is operative.

items.remarks:
(M)(O) May be inoperative provided:
a) Cabin differential pressure indicator is operative, and
b) A chart is available to the flightcrew which converts cabin differential pressure to cabin altitude.
(M)(O) May be inoperative provided flight is operated in an unpressurized configuration.

(M)(O) May be inoperative provided:
a) Cabin altimeter is operative, and
b) A chart is available to the flightcrew which converts cabin altitude to cabin differential pressure.
(M)(O) May be inoperative provided flight is operated in an unpressurized configuration.
(M)(O) May be inoperative provided cabin altimeter is operative.
(M)(O) May be inoperative provided flight is operated in an unpressurized configuration.

equipment.name: CCAS Cabin Altitude Warning (All models except ATR72-212A “600 Version”)

item.remarks: (O) May be inoperative provided flight remains at 10,000 ft. MSL or below.

equipment.name: Automatic Landing ELEV Display (ATR72-212A “600 Version” with Mod. 07696, ATR72-212A “600F Version” with Mod. 07696)

item.remarks: (O) May be inoperative provided Source SEL FMS MAN is verified operative.

equipment.name: Safety Valve (ATR72-212A “600 Version” with Mod. 07696, ATR72-212A “600F Version” with Mod. 07696)

items.remarks:
(M)(O) One may be inoperative provided:
a) Affected Safety Valve is secured in the open position, and
b) Associated Pack is considered inoperative and selected OFF.
Refer to Item 21-50-1, Packs.

(M)(O) Both may be inoperative provided:
a) Both Safety Valves are secured in the open position,
b) Both Packs are considered inoperative,
c) Operations are limited to one flight, and
d) Extended Operations are not conducted.
Refer to Item 21-50-1, Packs.

(M)(O) Both may be inoperative provided:
a) Flight is conducted in an unpressurized configuration with maximum operating altitude of 10,000 ft. MSL or below,
b) Both Safety Valves are secured in the open position,
c) One Pack & Bleed is verified operative, and
d) Extended Operations are not conducted.

items.detail: 1) (All models except ATR72-212A “600 Version” with Mod. 07696” and ATR72-212A “600F Version” with Mod. 07696)
items.remarks:
(O) One Pack may be inoperative provided:
a) Affected pack is selected OFF,
b) Flight is conducted at 17,000 ft. MSL or below, and
c) Extended Operations are not conducted.

items.detail: 2) (ATR72-212A “600 Version” with Mod. 07696, ATR72-212A “600F Version” with Mod. 07696)
items.remarks:
(O) One Pack may be inoperative provided:
a) Affected pack is selected OFF,
b) Flight is conducted at 17,000 ft. MSL or below, and
c) Extended Operations are not conducted.
NOTE: If PACK fault triggering cause is not identified by NAMS System Maintenance Advisory (SMA), refer to Item 21-50-2, Flow Control Valve (FCV).

items.detail: 2) (ATR72-212A “600 Version” with Mod. 07696, ATR72-212A “600F Version” with Mod. 07696)
items.remarks:
(M)(O) One Pack may be inoperative provided:
a) Affected pack is selected OFF,
b) Flight is conducted at 17,000 ft. MSL or below,
c) Extended Operations are not conducted,
d) Associated bleed valve is considered inoperative, and
e) GRD X FEED Valve is considered inoperative.
NOTE: If PACK fault triggering cause is not identified by NAMS System Maintenance Advisory (SMA), refer to Item 21-50-2, Flow Control Valve (FCV).
Refer to Item 36-11-2, Bleed Valves Systems.
Refer to Item 36-11-4, GRD X FEED Valve.

items.detail: 3) Passenger Configuration Only (ATR72-212A “600 Version” with Mod. 07696)
items.remarks:
(M)(O) Both PACKs may be inoperative provided:
a) Extended Operations are not conducted,
b) Both Packs are selected OFF,
c) Flight is conducted in an unpressurized configuration with maximum operating altitude of 10,000 ft. MSL or below,
d) Pressurization system is considered inoperative,
e) Emergency Ram Air Valve (ERAV) is verified operative, and
f) Operations are limited to one flight.
NOTE: If PACK fault triggering cause is not identified by NAMS System Maintenance Advisory (SMA), refer to Item 21-50-2, Flow Control Valve (FCV).
Refer to Item 21-30-1, Pressurization System.

items.detail: 3) Passenger Configuration Only (ATR72-212A “600 Version” with Mod. 07696)
items.remarks:
(M)(O) Both PACKs may be inoperative provided:
a) Extended Operations are not conducted,
b) Both Packs are selected OFF,
c) Flight is conducted in an unpressurized configuration with maximum operating altitude of 10,000 ft. MSL or below,
d) Pressurization system is considered inoperative,
e) Maximum flight time is limited to 80 minutes, and
f) Operations are limited to one flight.
NOTE: If PACK fault triggering cause is not identified by NAMS System Maintenance Advisory (SMA), refer to Item 21-50-2, Flow Control Valve (FCV).
Refer to Item 21-30-1, Pressurization System.

items.detail: 4) Cargo Configuration Only (ATR72-212A “600 Version” with Mod. 07696, ATR72-212A “600F Version” with Mod. 07696)
items.remarks:
(M)(O) Both PACKs may be inoperative provided:
a) Extended Operations are not conducted,
b) Both Packs are selected OFF,
c) Flight is conducted in an unpressurized configuration with maximum operating altitude of 10,000 ft. MSL or below,
d) Pressurization system is considered inoperative,
e) Emergency Ram Air Valve (ERAV) is verified operative,
f) Operations are limited to one flight, and
g) Procedures are established and used to ensure the cargo compartment remains empty or is verified to contain only empty cargo handling equipment, ballast (ballast may be loaded in ULDs), and/or Fly Away Kits.
NOTE 1: Operator MELs should define which items are approved for inclusion in the Fly Away Kits, and which materials can be used as ballast.
NOTE 2: If PACK fault triggering cause is not identified by NAMS System Maintenance Advisory (SMA), refer to item 21-50-2, Flow Control Valve (FCV).
Refer to Item 21-30-1, Pressurization System.

items.detail: 4) Cargo Configuration Only (ATR72-212A “600 Version” with Mod. 07696, ATR72-212A “600F Version” with Mod. 07696)
items.remarks:
(O) Both PACKs may be inoperative provided:
a) Extended Operations are not conducted,
b) Both Packs are selected OFF,
c) Flight is conducted in an unpressurized configuration with maximum operating altitude of 10,000 ft. MSL or below,
d) Pressurization system is considered inoperative,
e) Maximum flight time is limited to 80 minutes,
f) Operations are limited to one flight, and
g) Procedures are established and used to ensure the cargo compartment remains empty or is verified to contain only empty cargo handling equipment, ballast (ballast may be loaded in ULDs), and/or Fly Away Kits.
NOTE 1: Operator MELs should define which items are approved for inclusion in the Fly Away Kits, and which materials can be used as ballast.
NOTE 2: If PACK fault triggering cause is not identified by NAMS System Maintenance Advisory (SMA), refer to Item 21-50-2, Flow Control Valve (FCV).
Refer to Item 21-30-1, Pressurization System.

equipment.name: Flow Control Valve (ATR72-212A “600 Version” with Mod. 07696, ATR72-212A “600F Version” with Mod. 07696)

items.remarks:
(M)(O) One FCV may be inoperative provided:
a) Associated pack is considered inoperative and selected OFF,
b) Flight is conducted at 17,000 ft. MSL or below,
c) Extended Operations are not conducted, and
d) Affected Flow control Valve (FCV) is secured CLOSED.
Refer to Item 21-50-1, Packs.

(M)(O) Both FCVs may be inoperative provided:
a) Both Flow Control Valves (FCV) are secured CLOSED,
b) Both Packs are considered inoperative, and
c) Operations are limited to one flight.
Refer to Item 21-50-1, Packs.

items.detail: 1) Channel A (ATR72-212A “600 Version” with Mod. 07696, ATR72-212A “600F Version” with Mod. 07696)
items.remarks:
(M)(O) Channel A may be inoperative provided that:
a) Left Bleed is considered inoperative,
b) Left Pack is considered inoperative,
c) OUTFLOW “BK UP” automatic pressurization mode on CPCS is inoperative,
d) MAN SOURCE SEL on CPCS is inoperative,
e) Opposite channel is operative,
f) Flight is conducted at 17,000 ft. MSL or below,
g) OUTFLOW “NORM” automatic pressurization mode on CPCS is verified operative,
h) Right pack and bleed is verified operative, and
i) Emergency Ram Air Valve (ERAV) is verified operative.
Refer to Item 21-50-1, Packs.
Refer to Item 36-11-2, Bleed Valves Systems.
Refer to Item 21-31-2, Automatic Pressurization Control.

items.detail: 2) Channel B (ATR72-212A “600 Version” with Mod. 07696, ATR72-212A “600F Version” with Mod. 07696)
items.remarks:
(M)(O) Channel B may be inoperative provided that:
a) Right Bleed is considered inoperative,
b) Right Pack is considered inoperative,
c) OUTFLOW “NORM” automatic pressurization mode on CPCS is inoperative,
d) Opposite channel is operative,
e) Flight is conducted at 17,000 ft. MSL or below,
f) OUTFLOW “BK UP” automatic pressurization mode on CPCS is verified operative,
g) Left pack and bleed is verified operative, and
h) Emergency Ram Air Valve (ERAV) is verified operative.
Refer to Item 21-50-1, Packs.
Refer to Item 36-11-2, Bleed Valves Systems.
Refer to Item 21-31-2, Automatic Pressurization Control.

items.detail: 3) Channel A+B (Passenger Configuration Only) (ATR72-212A “600 Version” with Mod. 07696)
items.remarks:
(M)(O) Both channels may be inoperative provided:
a) Extended Operations are not conducted,
b) Both packs are considered inoperative and selected OFF,
c) Flight is conducted in an unpressurized configuration with maximum operating altitude of 10,000 ft. MSL or below,
d) Pressurization system is considered inoperative,
e) Emergency Ram Air Valve (ERAV) is verified operative, and
f) Operations are limited to one flight.

items.detail: 4) Channel A+B (Cargo Configuration Only) (ATR72-212A “600 Version” with Mod. 07696, ATR72-212A “600F Version” with Mod. 07696)
items.remarks:
(M)(O) Both channels may be inoperative provided:
a) Extended Operations are not conducted,
b) Both packs are considered inoperative and selected OFF,
c) Flight is conducted in an unpressurized configuration with maximum operating altitude of 10,000 ft. MSL or below,
d) Pressurization system is considered inoperative,
e) Emergency Ram Air Valve (ERAV) is verified operative,
f) Operations are limited to one flight, and
g) Procedures are established and used to ensure the cargo compartment remains empty or is verified to contain only empty cargo handling equipment, ballast (ballast may be loaded in ULDs), and/or Fly Away Kits.
NOTE: Operator MELs should define which items are approved for inclusion in the Fly Away Kits, and which materials can be used as ballast.
Refer to Item 21-50-1, Packs.
Refer to Item 21-30-1, Pressurization System.

One or both may be inoperative.

equipment.name: CARGO VENT - AIRFLOW Shut off Valve (Cargo Configuration) (ATR72-212A “600F Version”)

item.remarks:
May be inoperative provided procedures are established and used to ensure the cargo compartment remains empty, or is verified to contain empty cargo handling equipment, ballast (ballast may be loaded in ULDs), and/or Fly Away Kits.
NOTE: Operator MELs should define which non-combustible items are approved for inclusion in the Fly Away Kits, and which non-combustible materials can be used as ballast.
May be inoperative.
Autopilot function may be inoperative provided both TCS functions operate normally.
One or both may be inoperative.
(O) May be inoperative provided the pilot flying is on the selected side.

items.remarks:
One may be inoperative provided:
a) Autopilot is not used below 1,500 ft. AGL, and
b) Approach minimums do not require the use of the autopilot.
Both may be inoperative provided autopilot is not used.

May be inoperative provided:
a) Approach minimums do not require its use,
b) Enroute operations do not require its use,
c) Number flight segments and segment duration is acceptable to the flightcrew, and
d) Extended Operations are not conducted.
NOTE 1: Operators should make every effort to repair the autopilot early in the repair interval, as provided by this relief statement, in consideration of such factors as weather, traffic density, and the effect of other inoperative systems.
NOTE 2: Any mode which is operative may be used.

One may be inoperative provided:
a) Autopilot is not used below 1,500 ft. AGL, and
b) Approach minimums do not require the use of the autopilot.
Both may be inoperative provided autopilot is not used.

item.remarks: (O) May be inoperative provided procedures do not require its use.

items.remarks:
May be inoperative provided:
a) Rudder releasable centering unit is operative, and
b) Autopilot Function is considered inoperative.
Refer to Item 22-18-1, Autopilot Function.

May be inoperative provided:
a) One pilot monitors rudders with feet on pedals, and
b) Repairs are made within two flight legs.

Both may be inoperative provided:
a) Extended Operations are not conducted, and
b) Autopilot Function is not used.
Refer to Item 22-18-01, Autopilot Function.

equipment.name: Guidance Light (All models except ATR72-212A “600 Version”)

item.remarks: May be inoperative provided approach minimums do not require its use.

items.remarks: (O) May be inoperative provided IAS is operative.
May be inoperative provided FD is not used.
(O) May be inoperative provided ALT SEL knob is operative.
(O) May be inoperative provided VS mode with 0ft/min selection target is used for ALT HOLD.

(O) May be inoperative.

a) AP Basic Modes are operative, and
b) HDG knob and HDG bug are operative.
